item.title: Supreme Court checks Trump, but empowers the presidency
item.description: Despite losses on tariffs and birthright citizenship, the executive has emerged stronger

item.title: This is how we can make AI safe for everyone
item.description: The labs develop the technology, but citizens and their elected representatives must make the rules

item.title: Saks’ retail rebirth depends more on Gucci than Wall Street
item.description: Retailer has had to repair not only the balance sheet but also the rift with luxury brands it stocked

item.title: By attacking immigrants, South Africa is destroying itself
item.description: The latest outbreak of xenophobic violence is a betrayal of the nations that aided its freedom struggle

item.title: Burnham should beware uncanny echoes of the 1930s on defence
item.description: The UK’s new plan for military spending does not match up to the risks in an unstable world

item.title: Through saints and symbolic visits, Pope Leo gets his message across
item.description: The Vatican’s communication with the White House often requires no words

item.title: Trump’s contradictions mangle a complex history of US trade
item.description: The president’s obsession with tariffs reflects a misreading of the past 250 years

item.title: The job market is reaccelerating. Is the economy?
item.description: Plus the bitcoin clown car company crash

item.title: Divorce can mess up your pension (as well as your home life)
item.description: Asset splits are bedevilled by misconceptions — and women come off worst

item.title: Are we invulnerable or just plain lucky?
item.description: The fragility of the world’s economic resilience

item.title: Should I buy a leasehold flat or wait?
item.description: I’m worried about what might happen when the government bans the leasehold system

item.title: Erdoğan’s assault on democracy is a threat to Turkey’s allies
item.description: Authoritarian rule and imprisonment of political opponents could destabilise Nato and the region

item.title: What will murder the bull market?
item.description: As the investment adage goes, stock runs don’t die of old age

item.title: The Swiss are ‘sole-searching’ about a shoe
item.description: Debate over the On sportswear company raises questions about what should be labelled as a national product

item.title: AI jitters give Indian IPOs a moment to shine
item.description: Two Indian listings this year are expected to raise a combined $7bn

item.title: Space entrepreneurs borrow some surprisingly terrestrial tactics
item.description: Vertical integration helps to bridge the gap between today’s investments and returns that won’t come until later

item.title: Britain’s defence investment predicament
item.description: After months of wrangling, Starmer’s military funding plan still came up short

item.title: Supreme Court reminds America (and Trump) what citizenship is
item.description: The United States has always been based on the notion of welcoming newcomers from every country

item.title: Britain should put up more of a fight on the high cost of defence kit
item.description: A new mindset for procurement is needed as otherwise the big spending plan numbers amount to less than they promise

item.title: Tech CEOs want AI rules — it may be too late
item.description: A deregulated environment sounds good until it results in ad hoc political intervention

item.title: America’s stillborn 250th birthday
item.description: An event that was meant to celebrate an exceptional nation has been cornered by one man

item.title: Local government is a mess. Here is what Andy Burnham must decide
item.description: Prospective PM’s challenge will be getting his devolution proposals functioning by the next election

item.title: Figuring out the Fed in the ‘say less’ era
item.description: Plus, small-cap vulnerabilities

item.title: Is AI an exoskeleton for the mind?
item.description: Technology that helps people do things they couldn’t otherwise achieve can also lead to atrophy

item.title: What would multilateral ‘AI arms control’ look like?
item.description: Given the competition, it’s debatable whether a US-China safety deal is even possible
